summary: Audience tape with a touch of hiss and distance, yet it captures an electrifying second set highlighted by an off‑the‑charts The Other One and a monster Comes A Time jam.
review: The recording is an audience source that suffers from some hiss and a distant soundstage, but the performance more than makes up for those flaws.  Fans rave about the second‑set improvisations – especially The Other One, Comes A Time, and a soaring Sugaree – while noting the first set is merely competent.  Overall the show is considered one of the standout 1976 concerts despite the modest audio fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star ratings and praise the second‑set jams; recording issues are noted but not enough to outweigh the musical excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: A little hissy and distant, with a bass‑heavy subwoofer effect from the Orpheum speaker box; still listenable and enjoyable for fans despite not being a master soundboard.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid but not spectacular; second set erupts into a jam‑heavy marathon with a rare double encore (NFA) that gives the show its legendary status.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work, especially the flanger‑laced tone on The Other One, is repeatedly praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: Leads into Brown Eyed Women during the jam and shows strong bass presence in the second set transitions.
